channel.title: Libertas - Freedom Tech Signals
channel.description: Curated insights on freedom technology: Bitcoin, privacy tools, censorship resistance, and digital sovereignty.

item.title: Tails 7.4 Adds Persistent Language Settings, Updates Core Privacy Tools
item.description: Tails 7.4 introduces persistent language/keyboard settings for easier access while updating Tor Browser to 15.0.4 and dropping BitTorrent downloads due to security concerns.

item.title: 22 Rights Groups Urge UK PM to Confront China on Transnational Digital Repression
item.description: Freedom House and 21 other rights organizations called on UK PM to address China's transnational repression tactics including surveillance, harassment by proxy, and digital intimidation of UK-based activists and diaspora communities.

item.title: Filipino Journalist Sentenced 12-18 Years on Terrorism Charges Amid Evidence Planting Claims
item.description: Frenchie Mae Cumpio, 26-year-old Filipino journalist reporting on police/military abuses, sentenced to 12-18 years for terrorism financing after 5 years detention, with allegations authorities planted weapons during arrest.

item.title: Gabon Implements Nationwide Internet Blackout During 2023 Presidential Elections
item.description: Gabon cut internet access nationwide during August 2023 elections, with service restored after military coup. Government cited preventing violence and misinformation as justification.

item.title: OONI Documents Decade-Long Censorship of Women's Rights Sites in Iran
item.description: OONI measurements show Iran has systematically blocked women's rights websites since at least 2014, with repression intensifying despite 2022 Women, Life, Freedom protests.

item.title: Federal Judge Halts FBI Search of Washington Post Reporter's Seized Devices
item.description: Judge orders temporary stop to FBI search of devices seized from WaPo reporter Hannah Natanson's home in Pentagon leak investigation, citing First Amendment concerns.

item.title: Iran's Complete Internet Blackout Conceals Mass Violence Against 2,403+ Killed Protesters
item.description: Iran imposed total internet shutdown Jan 8, 2026 during nationwide protests, killing 2,403+ protesters and arresting 18,000+ while blocking all communications including satellite internet to conceal human rights abuses.

item.title: Bhutan Expands State Blockchain Portfolio with Sei Validator Node
item.description: Bhutan becomes validator on Sei blockchain, adding to existing Bitcoin mining operations and Ethereum-powered self-sovereign ID system in comprehensive state-level blockchain adoption.

item.title: EFF: Broken Copyright Damages Fuel Platform Censorship
item.description: EFF highlights how U.S. copyright's statutory damages ($200-$150k per work) create financial incentives for platforms to over-censor, stifling online expression and fair use.

item.title: EFF Guide to Navigating Age Verification Gates While Protecting Privacy
item.description: EFF releases practical guide for users forced to comply with age verification mandates, offering privacy-preserving strategies when faced with age gates on major platforms.
